name: Vector2f
summary: Represents the two components of a vector in two-dimensional space, using Single-precision floating point numbers.

methods.name: Add(Point2f, Vector2f)
methods.signature: public static Point2f Add(Point2f point, Vector2f vector)
methods.summary: Sums up a point and a vector, and returns a new point. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the + operator otherwise)

returns.doc: A new point that results from the addition of point and vector.

methods.name: Add(Vector2f, Vector2f)
methods.signature: public static Vector2f Add(Vector2f vector1, Vector2f vector2)
methods.summary: Sums up two vectors. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the + operator otherwise)

returns.doc: A new vector that results from the component-wise addition of the two vectors.

methods.name: CompareTo
methods.signature: public int CompareTo(Vector2f other)
methods.summary: Compares this Vector2f with another Vector2f. Components evaluation priority is first X, then Y.

params.name: other
params.doc: The other Vector2f to use in comparison.

returns.doc: 0: if this is identical to other-1: if this.X < other.X-1: if this.X == other.X and this.Y < other.Y+1: otherwise.

methods.name: EpsilonEquals
methods.signature: public bool EpsilonEquals(Vector2f other, float epsilon)
methods.summary: Check that all values in other are within epsilon of the values in this

returns.doc: [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.Vector2f.EpsilonEquals(Rhino.Geometry.Vector2f,System.Single)"]

methods.name: Equals(Object)
methods.signature: public override bool Equals(Object obj)
methods.summary: Determines whether the specified System.Object is a Vector2f and has the same values as the present vector.

returns.doc: true if obj is Vector2f and has the same coordinates as this; otherwise false.

methods.name: Equals(Vector2f)
methods.signature: public bool Equals(Vector2f vector)
methods.summary: Determines whether the specified vector has the same values as the present vector.

returns.doc: true if obj is Vector2f and has the same coordinates as this; otherwise false.

methods.name: GetHashCode
methods.signature: public override int GetHashCode()
methods.summary: Computes a hash number that represents the current vector.

returns.doc: A hash code that is not unique for each vector.

methods.name: Multiply
methods.signature: public static double Multiply(Vector2f point1, Vector2f point2)
methods.summary: Multiplies two Vector2f together, returning the dot (internal) product of the two. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the * operator otherwise)

returns.doc: A value that results from the coordinate-wise multiplication of point1 and point2.

methods.name: Negate
methods.signature: public static Vector2f Negate(Vector2f vector)
methods.summary: Computes the reversed vector. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the - unary operator otherwise)
methods.remarks: This is similar to Reverse(), but is static for CLR compliance, and with default name.

returns.doc: A new vector where all components were multiplied by -1.

methods.name: PerpendicularTo
methods.signature: public bool PerpendicularTo(Vector2f other)
methods.summary: Sets this vector to be perpendicular to another vector. Result is not unitized.

returns.doc: true on success, false if input vector is zero or invalid.

methods.name: Reverse
methods.signature: public bool Reverse()
methods.summary: Reverses this vector in place (reverses the direction). If this vector contains RhinoMath.UnsetValue, the reverse will also be invalid and false will be returned.
methods.remarks: Similar to Negate, that is only provided for CLR language compliance.

returns.doc: true on success or false if the vector is invalid.

methods.name: Subtract
methods.signature: public static Vector2f Subtract(Vector2f vector1, Vector2f vector2)
methods.summary: Subtracts the second vector from the first one. (Provided for languages that do not support operator overloading. You can use the - operator otherwise)

returns.doc: A new vector that results from the component-wise difference of vector1 - vector2.

methods.name: ToString()
methods.signature: public override string ToString()
methods.summary: Constructs the string representation of the current vector.

returns.doc: The vector representation in the form X,Y,Z.

methods.name: ToString(String, IFormatProvider)
methods.signature: public string ToString(string format, IFormatProvider formatProvider)
methods.summary: Formats the value of the current instance using the specified format.

params.name: format
params.doc: The format to use. -or- A null reference (null in Visual Basic) to use the default format defined for the type of the IFormattable implementation.

params.name: formatProvider
params.doc: The provider to use to format the value. -or- A null reference (null in Visual Basic) to obtain the numeric format information from the current locale setting of the operating system.

returns.doc: The value of the current instance in the specified format.

methods.name: Unitize
methods.signature: public bool Unitize()
methods.summary: Unitizes the vector in place. A unit vector has length 1 unit. An invalid or zero length vector cannot be unitized.

returns.doc: true on success or false on failure.

methods.name: Addition(Point2f, Vector2f)
methods.signature: public static Point2f operator +(Point2f point, Vector2f vector)
methods.summary: Sums up a point and a vector, and returns a new point.

returns.doc: A new point that results from the addition of point and vector.

methods.name: Addition(Vector2f, Vector2f)
methods.signature: public static Vector2f operator +(Vector2f a, Vector2f b)
methods.summary: Computes the sum between two vectors.

returns.doc: The difference.

methods.name: Equality
methods.signature: public static bool operator ==(Vector2f a, Vector2f b)
methods.summary: Determines whether two vectors have equal values.

returns.doc: true if the components of the two vectors are exactly equal; otherwise false.

methods.name: GreaterThan
methods.signature: public static bool operator >(Vector2f a, Vector2f b)
methods.summary: Determines whether the first specified vector comes after (has superior sorting value than) the second vector. Components have decreasing evaluation priority: first X, then Y.

returns.doc: true if a.X is larger than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y is larger than b.Y; otherwise, false.

methods.name: GreaterThanOrEqual
methods.signature: public static bool operator >=(Vector2f a, Vector2f b)
methods.summary: Determines whether the first specified vector comes after (has superior sorting value than) the second vector, or it is equal to it. Components have decreasing evaluation priority: first X, then Y.

returns.doc: true if a.X is larger than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y >= b.Y; otherwise, false.

methods.name: Inequality
methods.signature: public static bool operator !=(Vector2f a, Vector2f b)
methods.summary: Determines whether two vectors have different values.

returns.doc: true if the two vectors differ in any component; false otherwise.

methods.name: LessThan
methods.signature: public static bool operator <(Vector2f a, Vector2f b)
methods.summary: Determines whether the first specified vector comes before (has inferior sorting value than) the second vector. Components have decreasing evaluation priority: first X, then Y.

returns.doc: true if a.X is smaller than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y is smaller than b.Y; otherwise, false.

methods.name: LessThanOrEqual
methods.signature: public static bool operator <=(Vector2f a, Vector2f b)
methods.summary: Determines whether the first specified vector comes before (has inferior sorting value than) the second vector, or it is equal to it. Components have decreasing evaluation priority: first X, then Y.

returns.doc: true if a.X is smaller than b.X, or a.X == b.X and a.Y <= b.Y; otherwise, false.

methods.name: Multiply
methods.signature: public static double operator *(Vector2f point1, Vector2f point2)
methods.summary: Multiplies two Vector2f together, returning the dot (internal) product of the two.

returns.doc: A value that results from the coordinate-wise multiplication of point1 and point2.

methods.name: Subtraction
methods.signature: public static Vector2f operator -(Vector2f a, Vector2f b)
methods.summary: Computes the difference between two vectors.

returns.doc: The difference.

methods.name: UnaryNegation
methods.signature: public static Vector2f operator -(Vector2f vector)
methods.summary: Computes the opposite vector.

returns.doc: A new vector where all components were multiplied by -1.

properties.name: IsUnitVector
properties.summary: Gets a value indicating whether or not this is a unit vector. A unit vector has length 1.

properties.name: IsValid
properties.summary: Returns an indication regarding the validity of this vector.

properties.name: IsZero
properties.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the X, Y, and Z values are all equal to 0.0.

properties.name: Length
properties.summary: Computes the length (or magnitude, or size) of this vector. This is an application of Pythagoras' theorem. If this vector is invalid, its length is considered 0.

properties.name: SquareLength
properties.summary: Returns the square of the length of this vector. This method does not check for the validity of its inputs.

properties.name: Unset
properties.summary: Gets the value of the vector with each component set to RhinoMath.UnsetValue.

properties.name: X
properties.summary: Gets or sets the X (first) component of this vector.

properties.name: XAxis
properties.summary: Gets the value of the vector with components 1,0.

properties.name: Y
properties.summary: Gets or sets the Y (second) component of this vector.

properties.name: YAxis
properties.summary: Gets the value of the vector with components 0,1.

properties.name: Zero
properties.summary: Gets the value of the vector with components 0,0.
